metadata.title: Build a Task-Brief Validator with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Hola, Liam — in for Bear. A task brief with a missing Exclusions field is an implicit permission slip for the AI to decide something the human thought was decided? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: A task brief with a missing Exclusions field is an implicit permission slip for the AI to decide something the human thought was decided?
props.segment: Build a Task-Brief Validator with Claude

beats.narration_text: A task brief with a missing Exclusions field is an implicit permission slip for the AI to decide something the human thought was decided.

The 7-field CoWork template exists because delegation requires an interface contract — Objective, Allowed Sources, Exclusions, Output Artifact, Decision Rules, Review Checkpoints, Stop Conditions. Missing any one field creates an ambiguity that surfaces only when the output is wrong.
Score this task brief against the 7-field CoWork template. Flag each missing or underspecified field with an explanation of why that gap will cause problems.

props.command: claude "Read this task brief and score it against the 7-field CoWork template. Flag each missing or underspecified field with an explanation of why that gap will cause problems." < brief.md

beats.narration_text: The scoring script loops over all 7 fields, checks presence and specificity, and produces a scored report with a delegation-readiness rating.

Validator scores a 4-field brief: 3 fields flagged missing — Exclusions, Stop Conditions, Review Checkpoints. Delegation readiness: 57 percent — NOT READY. Each missing field gets a one-line explanation of what the AI will decide in its absence.
Add a second brief with all 7 fields but vague Decision Rules: use judgment.

props.command: claude "Score this revised brief. Decision Rules field says: use judgment." < brief_v2.md

beats.narration_text: All 7 fields present — completeness 100 percent. But specificity score: 71 percent. Decision Rules flagged: use judgment is not a decision rule; it's a delegation of judgment itself. Completeness does not equal quality.

The 7-field template is an interface contract. Every missing field is an implicit permission slip — the AI decides something the human assumed was decided. Completeness checks presence; specificity checks whether the field actually constrains the AI.
Next: measure which tasks belong to Claude versus human with a routing matrix.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Next: measure which tasks belong to Claude versus human with a routing matrix. Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Build a Task-Brief Validator with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
